|timeline=[[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]<ref name="Vader-Quest">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates ''[[Rebel Force: Target]]'' to 2 months after the Battle of Yavin, and also dates ''[[Star Wars: Vader's Quest]]'' and [[Star Wars (1977) 35|''Star Wars'' (1977) 35]]—in which Vader discovers and acts upon Luke Skywalker's identity, respectively—to immediately before ''Trapped''. This lines up with the date provided for ''Vader's Quest'' in ''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Early Victories]]'', and thus dates them both to [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] with ''Trapped''.</ref>
|series=[[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'' (1977)]]
|issue=37

{{Quote|I've thoroughly ''tested'' you... made you perform in a hostile environment... probed your emotions by placing your friends in jeopardy. I now know your limits... and your limitations!|Darth Vader|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 37">[[Star Wars (1977) 37|''Star Wars'' (1977) 37]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars 37: In Mortal Combat''''' is the thirty-seventh issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics.

==Plot summary==
[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] enter the dangerous [[Crystal Valley (Monastery)|Crystal Valley]] as arranged by [[Domina Tagge/Legends|Domina Tagge]], her plan is to not only have Luke killed, but Vader as well since he was a long time rival of her brother [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Orman Tagge/Legends|Orman Tagge]]. The [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] arrives on the planet on the order of Baron Tagge, and he enters the Crystal Valley to confront Vader. Vader reveals to Orman that his sister is not as innocent as the eldest Tagge would believe and convinces him to duel Luke in Vader's place. Orman does so, but Vader makes a visual illusion so that Luke believes he is really fighting Vader. Luke kills Orman and escapes the Crystal Valley, and is reunited with his [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] comrades.

==Continuity==
The last page of this issue shows "[[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]" reinstating [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] debt to him after he forgave it in [[Star Wars (1977) 28|''Star Wars'' (1977) 28]]. He is, however, erroneously illustrated. The article "[[Red Five (department)|Red Five]]" in [[Star Wars Insider 149|''Star Wars Insider'' 149]] establishes that the "Jabba" in the Marvel comics is in fact Jabba's [[Nimbanel/Legends|Nimbanel]] accountant [[Mosep Binneed/Legends|Mosep Binneed]], who sometimes used Jabba's name in interactions.
